title: Define a class
instruction:
A class has fields (data) and methods (behaviour).

Run this.
successMessage: new Dog() creates an instance. dot notation accesses fields and methods. public means accessible from outside the class.

title: Constructor
instruction:
A constructor initialises an object when created with `new`.

Run this.
successMessage: The constructor runs when you call new Point(3, 4). It receives the arguments and initialises the fields.

title: this keyword
instruction:
`this` refers to the current instance. Use it when parameter names shadow field names.

Run this.
successMessage: this.name refers to the field. name (without this) refers to the parameter. Without this, parameter shadows the field.

title: Multiple constructors
instruction:
A class can have multiple constructors with different parameters (overloading).

Run this.
successMessage: Constructor chaining with : this(...) reuses another constructor. The square constructor calls the full constructor with equal width and height.

title: Encapsulation with private
instruction:
Use `private` fields with `public` methods to protect internal state.

Run this.
successMessage: balance is private — code outside cannot set it directly. Deposit and Withdraw enforce the business rules. This is encapsulation.

title: Static members
instruction:
Static fields and methods belong to the class, not instances.

Run this.
successMessage: count is static — shared across all instances. Every new Counter() increments the same count. Static members are accessed via the class name.

title: ToString override
instruction:
Override `ToString()` to control how your object prints.

Run this.
successMessage: Overriding ToString() controls what Console.WriteLine and string interpolation display. Essential for debugging and logging.

title: Object initialiser syntax
instruction:
Set public properties in a `{}` block after the constructor call.

Run this.
successMessage: Object initialisers are clean and readable, especially for configuration or DTO objects. No constructor needed.
